title: Koanophyllon microchaeteum
intro: Koanophyllon microchaeteum is a plant species that belongs to the Asteraceae family, commonly known as a type of daisy or sunflower. The plant is reported to have several health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help in treating various health conditions such as wounds and infections. The bioactive constituents of Koanophyllon microchaeteum include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Koanophyllon microchaeteum is often used in traditional medicine and is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as being incorporated into topical ointments and creams for wound care.
taxonomy: Koanophyllon microchaeteum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon and is native to Mexico. Within its classification, Koanophyllon microchaeteum is further divided into the tribe Neuwiedieae and the subfamily Asteroideae. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and taxonomic position. The specific classification of Koanophyllon microchaeteum provides valuable information for botanists and researchers.
